name: Singles Tempo Push
description: Extended single stroke roll sessions focused on building clean speed through relaxation

teacher_notes.beginner: Speed is a result of relaxation, not effort. If you feel tension in your forearms, you're going too fast.
teacher_notes.intermediate: At higher tempos, switch from wrist strokes to a combination of wrist and finger technique.
teacher_notes.advanced: Record yourself. Tension shows up in video even when you can't feel it. Watch your shoulders and jaw.

name: Doubles Speed Builder
description: Building clean double stroke speed through controlled tempo progression

teacher_notes.beginner: At slow tempos, each double is two deliberate strokes. At fast tempos, it's one controlled bounce.
teacher_notes.intermediate: The crossover point — where stroke technique becomes bounce technique — is usually around 120–140 BPM. Find yours.
teacher_notes.advanced: Open rolls require the bounce to be perfectly controlled. Practice at the crossover point until it's seamless.
